title: claude-kit project manifest
description: Every project-specific value the generic orchestration kit reads. Fill this; touch no kit file.

law_doc.description: Path to the root law document (e.g. CLAUDE.md).

module_law_docs.description: module path -> its own law doc (e.g. DB rules in the schema-owning module).

lang_codes.description: Domain language/locale codes that must NOT be hardcoded in source.

class_m_triggers.description: File-path patterns that force Mandatory-Opus + risk assessment.

match.description: Python regex against the file path.

law_patterns.description: Forbidden-pattern checks run by post-edit-laws.py on the changed file.

glob.description: fnmatch against the file basename, e.g. '*.sql'.

forbid.description: Python regex; a match is a violation.

sample_violation.description: Optional: a string that violates `forbid`, used by doctor.py for a live block-test.

worktree_guard.description: Working-tree loss guard thresholds (P2). Tier A (_worktree.py, WARN-only on Stop/SessionStart) alarms on a bulk deletion vs HEAD or a module root emptied past a ratio; Tier B (pre-bash-guard.py, non-blocking) flags whole-tree destructive shell commands. Content stays safe in HEAD — the guard never mutates the tree; it only warns + gives recovery commands.

bulk_delete_threshold.description: Tier A alarm: total tracked files deleted vs HEAD at/above this count triggers the WARN, regardless of module distribution.

module_root_empty_ratio.description: Tier A alarm: a declared module root is flagged EMPTIED when this fraction (0..1) or more of its HEAD-tracked files are deleted in the working tree.

destructive_bash.description: Tier B: Python regexes matched against a Bash command; a match injects a non-blocking reminder. Whole-tree catastrophic forms ONLY (recursive-force rm, git reset --hard, checkout/restore/clean of '.', worktree remove/prune) — never plain rm or branch checkout. Omit to use pre-bash-guard.py's built-in default list.

resume_marker.description: How session-start.py detects stale resume state vs the repo.

regex.description: Extracts the brief's claimed version, group(1)=number.

repo_glob.description: Glob (slash-separated) for the artifacts that advance that version.

repo_regex.description: Extracts the version number from a matched path.

code_globs.description: Production-code extensions for the learning-note reminder.

work_dir.description: Work-board workspace dir (kanban: PROCESS.md, BOARD.md, items/). Default 'work'.

kit_version.description: Kit VERSION this manifest was written against (informational).

hygiene.description: Prevention protocol: bloat_limits (lines per extension; warn at limit, BLOCK at limit×hard_factor), hard_factor, claude_sanctioned_dirs (structure guard).

modules.description: Project module paths (e.g. 'apps/backend'); bootstrap generates a per-module specialist for each.

shared_lib_root.description: Root dir of shared, project-agnostic libraries (e.g. 'libs'); arch rules forbid these from importing app packages.
